---
weight: 25
title: SonarQube Connector Automatic Creation Tekton Task
---

# SonarQube Connector Automatic Creation Tekton Task

A Tekton Task that provisions and maintains a **tenant-scoped SonarQube
Connector** on the cluster in a single run. The Task creates (or reuses)
the SonarQube-side identity, scopes the tenant's project keyspace, mints
a refreshable user token, and writes the tenant `sonarqube` Connector
plus its token Secret to the target namespace.

The Task is idempotent and safe to schedule on a recurring basis: the
user token is refreshed on every run and auto-renewed before expiry.

For the operator walkthrough — prerequisites, onboarding, rotation, and
offboarding — see
[Using the Task](./using_sonarqube_connector_automatic_creation_task.mdx).

## Installation \{#installation}

The connectors-operator installs only the SonarQube framework (CRDs,
RBAC, ConnectorClass, ResourceInterface). The Task itself is
user-applied per Tekton catalog convention.

Pick a registry mirror reachable from the cluster and the namespace
where you want the Task installed, then apply the manifest below. The
generated block is kept in sync from
`cmd/kodata/connectors-sonarqube-tektoncd/<version>/install.yaml` by
`hack/sync_sonarqube_connector_automatic_creation_task_doc.sh`.

- `REGISTRY`: the registry that hosts the
  `sonarqube-connector-automatic-creation` tool image (and any mirrored
  catalog images). For example `registry.alauda.cn:60070`.
- `TARGET_NS`: the namespace where you want to install the Task.

{/* BEGIN GENERATED TASK YAML */}
```bash
export REGISTRY=<your-registry>
export TARGET_NS=<target-namespace>

cat <<'EOF' | envsubst '${REGISTRY} ${TARGET_NS}' | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: tekton.dev/v1
kind: Task
metadata:
  annotations:
    style.tekton.dev/descriptors: |
      - path: params.connector
        x-descriptors:
          - urn:alm:descriptor:label:en:Connector
          - 'urn:alm:descriptor:description:en:Target tenant SonarQube Connector to create or update, in <namespace>/<name> format, for example connectors-management/acme-sonarqube'
          - 'urn:alm:descriptor:tooltip:en:The token Secret is created in the same namespace as the Connector'
          - urn:alm:descriptor:com.tectonic.ui:text
          - urn:alm:descriptor:com.tectonic.ui:validation:required
      - path: params.tenant
        x-descriptors:
          - urn:alm:descriptor:label:en:Tenant
          - 'urn:alm:descriptor:description:en:Tenant identifier. Used to derive default names for the SonarQube user and permission template when those fields are left empty'
          - urn:alm:descriptor:com.tectonic.ui:text
          - urn:alm:descriptor:com.tectonic.ui:validation:required
      - path: params.projectPattern
        x-descriptors:
          - urn:alm:descriptor:label:en:Project Key Pattern
          - 'urn:alm:descriptor:description:en:Regular expression matching the tenant project key space. Used to scope the permission template so it only applies to this tenant. Must not overlap with the patterns used by other tenants'
          - 'urn:alm:descriptor:tooltip:en:Example: ^acme-.* to scope this tenant to project keys starting with acme-'
          - urn:alm:descriptor:com.tectonic.ui:text
          - urn:alm:descriptor:com.tectonic.ui:validation:required
      - path: params.templatePermissions
        x-descriptors:
          - urn:alm:descriptor:label:en:Template Permissions
          - 'urn:alm:descriptor:description:en:Project-level permissions granted to the tenant user. The default covers Browse, See Source Code, and Execute Analysis. Do not include admin, otherwise the tenant could change project visibility or delete projects'
          - urn:alm:descriptor:com.tectonic.ui:text-array
      - path: params.tokenDuration
        x-descriptors:
          - urn:alm:descriptor:label:en:Token Duration (days)
          - 'urn:alm:descriptor:description:en:Validity of the SonarQube user token, in whole days (positive integer). The token is automatically renewed before it expires on each run, so the Task can be scheduled on a recurring basis'
          - urn:alm:descriptor:com.tectonic.ui:text
          - urn:alm:descriptor:com.tectonic.ui:validation:required
    style.tekton.dev/displayParams: connector,tenant,projectPattern,templatePermissions,tokenDuration
    tekton.dev/categories: CLI
    tekton.dev/displayName: SonarQube Connector Automatic Creation
    tekton.dev/icon: data:image/svg+xml;base64,
    tekton.dev/pipelines.minVersion: 0.56.0
    tekton.dev/platforms: linux/amd64,linux/arm64
    tekton.dev/tags: sonarqube, connector, kubernetes, secret, user-token
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/version: "0.1"
  name: sonarqube-connector-automatic-creation
  namespace: ${TARGET_NS}
spec:
  description: Automatically provisions and maintains a tenant-scoped SonarQube Connector in one TaskRun. For the given tenant, the Task creates or reuses a dedicated SonarQube user, a permission template scoped to the tenant's project key pattern, and a user token; then creates or updates the tenant SonarQube Connector together with its token Secret. SonarQube projects are not created upfront -- they are auto-created at scan time by the tenant user. The user token is refreshed on every run and auto-renewed before expiry, so the Task is safe to schedule on a recurring basis.
  params:
    - description: Target tenant SonarQube Connector to create or update, in <namespace>/<name> format, for example connectors-management/acme-sonarqube. The token Secret is created in the same namespace as the Connector
      name: connector
      type: string
    - description: Tenant identifier. Used to derive default names for the SonarQube user and permission template when those fields are left empty
      name: tenant
      type: string
    - default: ""
      description: Optional SonarQube server URL, for example https://sonarqube.example.com. Set this to override the address provided by the sonarqube-config workspace; leave empty to use the workspace value
      name: sonarqubeUrl
      type: string
    - description: Regular expression matching the tenant project key space. Used to scope the permission template so it only applies to this tenant. Must not overlap with the patterns used by other tenants
      name: projectPattern
      type: string
    - default: ""
      description: Name of the SonarQube permission template to create or reuse. Leave empty to use <tenant>-template
      name: permissionTemplate
      type: string
    - default:
        - user
        - codeviewer
        - scan
      description: Project-level permissions granted to the tenant user. The default covers Browse, See Source Code, and Execute Analysis. Do not include 'admin', otherwise the tenant could change project visibility or delete projects
      name: templatePermissions
      type: array
    - default: ""
      description: SonarQube user login to create or reuse. Leave empty to use <tenant>-bot
      name: userName
      type: string
    - default: "30"
      description: Validity of the SonarQube user token, in whole days (positive integer). The token is automatically renewed before it expires on each run, so the Task can be scheduled on a recurring basis
      name: tokenDuration
      type: string
    - default: ${REGISTRY}/devops/sonarqube-connector-automatic-creation:v1.9.0-g8632398
      description: Tool image used by every step. The default points at the release image published by this repository; override for air-gapped clusters or to pin a specific version
      name: sonarqubeAutomationImage
      type: string
    - default: Always
      description: Controls when Kubernetes pulls the tool image
      name: imagePullPolicy
      type: string
    - default: "false"
      description: Enable verbose logging for debugging. Credentials and tokens are never logged regardless of this flag
      name: verbose
      type: string
  results:
    - description: Tenant identifier handled by this run
      name: tenant
    - description: SonarQube user login created or reused by this run
      name: username
    - description: SonarQube permission template name created or reused by this run
      name: permission-template
    - description: Name of the SonarQube user token created or reused by this run. NEVER exposes the token value as a result; the value is written only to the tenant Secret
      name: token-name
    - description: Reference of the tenant Connector created or updated by this run, in <namespace>/<name> format
      name: connector-ref
  stepTemplate:
    securityContext:
      allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
      capabilities:
        drop:
          - ALL
      runAsGroup: 65532
      runAsNonRoot: true
      runAsUser: 65532
      seccompProfile:
        type: RuntimeDefault
    volumeMounts:
      - mountPath: /workspace/state
        name: state
      - mountPath: /workspace/secrets
        name: secrets
  steps:
    - args:
        - $(params.templatePermissions[*])
      computeResources:
        limits:
          cpu: 500m
          memory: 256Mi
        requests:
          cpu: 100m
          memory: 128Mi
      env:
        - name: TENANT
          value: $(params.tenant)
        - name: CONNECTOR_REF
          value: $(params.connector)
        - name: SONARQUBE_URL
          value: $(params.sonarqubeUrl)
        - name: PROJECT_PATTERN
          value: $(params.projectPattern)
        - name: PERMISSION_TEMPLATE
          value: $(params.permissionTemplate)
        - name: USER_NAME
          value: $(params.userName)
        - name: TOKEN_DURATION_DAYS
          value: $(params.tokenDuration)
        - name: VERBOSE
          value: $(params.verbose)
        - name: SONAR_CONFIG_DIR
          value: $(workspaces.sonarqube-config.path)
        - name: STATE_DIR
          value: /workspace/state
        - name: SECRETS_DIR
          value: /workspace/secrets
      image: $(params.sonarqubeAutomationImage)
      imagePullPolicy: $(params.imagePullPolicy)
      name: ensure-tenant
      script: |
        #!/usr/bin/env bash
        ensure-tenant.sh "$@"
    - computeResources:
        limits:
          cpu: 500m
          memory: 256Mi
        requests:
          cpu: 100m
          memory: 128Mi
      env:
        - name: TENANT
          value: $(params.tenant)
        - name: CONNECTOR_REF
          value: $(params.connector)
        - name: SONARQUBE_URL
          value: $(params.sonarqubeUrl)
        - name: VERBOSE
          value: $(params.verbose)
        - name: SONAR_CONFIG_DIR
          value: $(workspaces.sonarqube-config.path)
        - name: STATE_DIR
          value: /workspace/state
        - name: SECRETS_DIR
          value: /workspace/secrets
        - name: WORKSPACE_KUBECONFIG_BOUND
          value: $(workspaces.kubeconfig.bound)
        - name: WORKSPACE_KUBECONFIG_PATH
          value: $(workspaces.kubeconfig.path)
      image: $(params.sonarqubeAutomationImage)
      imagePullPolicy: $(params.imagePullPolicy)
      name: apply-kubernetes-resources
      script: |
        #!/usr/bin/env bash
        apply-step.sh
    - computeResources:
        limits:
          cpu: 100m
          memory: 128Mi
        requests:
          cpu: 50m
          memory: 64Mi
      env:
        - name: TENANT
          value: $(params.tenant)
        - name: CONNECTOR_REF
          value: $(params.connector)
        - name: PERMISSION_TEMPLATE
          value: $(params.permissionTemplate)
        - name: USER_NAME
          value: $(params.userName)
        - name: VERBOSE
          value: $(params.verbose)
        - name: RESULT_TENANT_PATH
          value: $(results.tenant.path)
        - name: RESULT_USERNAME_PATH
          value: $(results.username.path)
        - name: RESULT_PERMISSION_TEMPLATE_PATH
          value: $(results.permission-template.path)
        - name: RESULT_TOKEN_NAME_PATH
          value: $(results.token-name.path)
        - name: RESULT_CONNECTOR_REF_PATH
          value: $(results.connector-ref.path)
      image: $(params.sonarqubeAutomationImage)
      imagePullPolicy: $(params.imagePullPolicy)
      name: write-results
      script: |
        #!/usr/bin/env bash
        write-results.sh
  volumes:
    - emptyDir:
        medium: Memory
      name: state
    - emptyDir:
        medium: Memory
      name: secrets
  workspaces:
    - description: |
        SonarQube admin access for the Task. Bind this workspace to a
        SonarQube ConnectorClass configuration (recommended), or to a
        Secret that provides the SonarQube address plus either an admin
        token or a username/password pair. Credentials are read from
        the workspace and never appear in the Pod spec, environment
        variables, or process arguments.
      name: sonarqube-config
    - description: |
        Optional. Bind this workspace to a kubeconfig when the tenant
        Connector and its Secret need to be applied to a different
        cluster than the one running the Task. Leave unbound to apply
        to the current cluster.
      name: kubeconfig
      optional: true
EOF
```
{/* END GENERATED TASK YAML */}

Notes:

- The default `sonarqubeAutomationImage` lives at
  `${REGISTRY}/devops/sonarqube-connector-automatic-creation:v1.9.0-g8632398`. Override the
  `sonarqubeAutomationImage` param at TaskRun time for air-gapped clusters or to
  pin a specific version.
- The Task pod runs as non-root (`runAsUser: 65532`) with
  `RuntimeDefault` seccomp, no capabilities, and tmpfs-backed
  `state` / `secrets` volumes.

## Description

Given a tenant identifier and a project key pattern, the Task:

1. Creates (or reuses) a dedicated **local SonarQube user**
   `<tenant>-bot`.
2. Grants that user the global `provisioning` permission so the tenant
   can auto-create projects matching its pattern at scan time.
3. Creates (or reuses) a **permission template** scoped to the
   tenant's `projectKeyPattern`, with project-level permissions
   (`user`, `codeviewer`, `scan` by default) bound **directly to the
   tenant user** — not via any group.
4. Mints (or reuses) a `USER_TOKEN` with a configurable validity window
   (`tokenDuration` days; default 30).
5. Writes a tenant `sonarqube` **Connector** with `tokenAuth` plus a
   matching **Secret** (`connectors.cpaas.io/bearer-token`) carrying
   the freshly-minted token.

Steps 1–3 are write-once; step 4 is reused while the token is still
valid and the tenant Secret still holds a non-empty token value.

The Task does **not** pre-create any SonarQube projects. Projects are
auto-created at scan time by the tenant user — the
`projectKeyPattern` on the tenant template is what restricts that
auto-creation to the tenant's namespace.

### Why a dedicated user (not a group)

SonarQube permission templates can be bound either to a user or to a
group. This Task uses the **direct-to-user** binding. The reason is
external-provisioning compatibility: when SCIM / GitHub / GitLab
provisioning is enabled on the SonarQube instance, group membership
for non-IDP users is wiped on every sync, which would silently revoke
the tenant's project permissions. Binding permissions directly to the
local tenant user side-steps the group indirection. See
[Using the Task — Local user creation must be allowed](./using_sonarqube_connector_automatic_creation_task.mdx#local-user-creation).

## Parameters

| Name | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `connector` | string | _required_ | Target tenant SonarQube Connector to create or update, in `<namespace>/<name>` format (for example `acme-prod/acme-sonarqube`). The token Secret is created in the same namespace. |
| `tenant` | string | _required_ | Tenant identifier. Used to derive default names for the SonarQube user (`<tenant>-bot`) and permission template (`<tenant>-template`) when those fields are left empty. |
| `sonarqubeUrl` | string | `""` | Optional SonarQube server URL (for example `https://sonarqube.example.com`). When set, overrides the address provided by the `sonarqube-config` workspace; leave empty to use the workspace value. Useful when the same admin credentials are reused across multiple SonarQube instances. |
| `projectPattern` | string | _required_ | Regular expression matching the tenant project key space (for example `^acme(:.*)?$`). Used to scope the permission template so it only applies to this tenant. **Must not overlap with patterns used by other tenants.** |
| `permissionTemplate` | string | `""` | Name of the SonarQube permission template to create or reuse. Leave empty to use `<tenant>-template`. |
| `templatePermissions` | array | `[user, codeviewer, scan]` | Project-level permissions granted to the tenant user via the template. Covers Browse, See Source Code, and Execute Analysis. **Do not include `admin`** — the tenant could otherwise change project visibility or delete projects. |
| `userName` | string | `""` | SonarQube user login to create or reuse. Leave empty to use `<tenant>-bot`. |
| `tokenDuration` | string | `"30"` | Validity of the SonarQube user token, in whole days (positive integer). The token is auto-renewed before it expires on each run, so the Task can be scheduled on a recurring basis. |
| `sonarqubeAutomationImage` | string | `registry.alauda.cn:60070/devops/sonarqube-connector-automatic-creation:v1.9.0-g8632398` | Tool image used by every step. Override for air-gapped clusters or to pin a specific version. |
| `imagePullPolicy` | string | `Always` | Controls when Kubernetes pulls the tool image. |
| `verbose` | string | `"false"` | Enable verbose logging for debugging. Credentials and tokens are never logged regardless of this flag. |

## Workspaces

| Name | Required | Description |
|------|----------|-------------|
| `sonarqube-config` | yes | SonarQube admin access for the Task. Bind to a SonarQube ConnectorClass configuration (recommended) or to a Secret that provides the SonarQube address plus either an admin token or a username/password pair. Credentials are read from the workspace and never appear in the Pod spec, environment variables, or process arguments. See [Workspace binding modes](#workspace-binding-modes) below. |
| `kubeconfig` | no | Bind to a kubeconfig when the tenant Connector and its Secret need to be applied to a **different cluster** than the one running the Task. Leave unbound to apply to the current cluster. |

### Workspace binding modes \{#workspace-binding-modes}

The `sonarqube-config` workspace is auto-detected. Pick the binding
that best matches how the admin Connector is exposed in your cluster:

**Mode A — Secret-direct (bootstrap / cross-cluster).**
Bind `sonarqube-config` to a Secret whose data carries credentials
only — one of:

- `token` — a SonarQube admin token (recommended), **or**
- `username` + `password` — admin Basic-auth credentials

Pass the SonarQube base URL via the `sonarqubeUrl` parameter at
TaskRun time. The Task reads the workspace files at runtime and uses
curl's `--config` shape, so the admin credential never appears in the
Pod environment or argv. The same Secret can target multiple SonarQube
instances by varying the `sonarqubeUrl` param.

**Mode B — Connectors CSI Driver (recommended for the day-2 path).**
Bind `sonarqube-config` to a volume projected by the
[Connectors CSI Driver](../../connectors/concepts/connectors_csi.mdx)
from the SonarQube `sonar-scanner` ConnectorClass configuration. The
Task reads `sonar.host.url` plus the `sonar.scanner.proxy*` routing
fields from the projected `sonar-project.properties` and routes every
API call through the
[Connectors Proxy](../../connectors/concepts/connectors_proxy.mdx),
which injects the SonarQube `Authorization` header from the admin
Connector. The admin token never lands in the Task pod.

## Results

| Name | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `tenant` | Tenant identifier handled by this run. |
| `username` | SonarQube user login created or reused by this run (defaults to `<tenant>-bot`). |
| `permission-template` | SonarQube permission template name created or reused (defaults to `<tenant>-template`). |
| `token-name` | Name of the SonarQube user token created or reused by this run. **The token value is never emitted as a result** — it is written only to the tenant Secret. |
| `connector-ref` | Reference of the tenant Connector, in `<namespace>/<name>` format. |

## Steps

The Task runs three steps in order:

| Step | Side-effects | Purpose |
|------|--------------|---------|
| `ensure-tenant` | SonarQube Web API + tmpfs `state` / `secrets` | SonarQube-side preflight + provisioning. Verifies the admin identity holds **Administer System** (prerequisite [P5](./using_sonarqube_connector_automatic_creation_task.mdx#prerequisites)), then creates/reuses the user, grants `provisioning`, creates/reuses the permission template with `templatePermissions`, and mints/reuses the user token. Writes the token to the tmpfs `secrets` volume. A `trap` on this step revokes the just-minted token and tears down newly-created template / `provisioning` grant / user on any non-zero exit. |
| `apply-kubernetes-resources` | Kubernetes API on the target cluster | Cluster-side write. Reads the token from the tmpfs volume and applies the tenant `sonarqube` Connector + Secret via Server-Side Apply with field manager `connector-auto`. Applies to the local cluster by default, or to the cluster identified by the `kubeconfig` workspace when bound. |
| `write-results` | TaskRun result paths + tmpfs cleanup | Writes the five Task results from the in-memory state file built by step 1, then clears `/workspace/secrets/token` so the freshly-minted token does not survive in the Pod's tmpfs beyond the run. |

The first step uses tmpfs volumes (`emptyDir.medium: Memory`) for both
state and secrets so credentials never touch the node filesystem.

### Failure handling

Failures are scoped per step:

- **`ensure-tenant` fails.** Its `trap` handler revokes **only the token
  this run minted**, and deletes only the template / `provisioning`
  grant / user that this run created — anything reused from a prior
  run is left intact. The TaskRun exits non-zero.
- **`apply-kubernetes-resources` fails** (e.g. SSA rejected, kubeconfig
  invalid, RBAC missing). The SonarQube side is **not** rolled back —
  the user, template, and token remain. The TaskRun exits non-zero;
  the next run self-heals: it sees `user_tokens/search` hit but a
  missing or empty tenant Secret and re-mints + re-applies.
- **`write-results` fails.** SonarQube state and the cluster Secret /
  Connector are already correct; only the Task results are missing.
  Re-running is a no-op for the side-effecting state.

## Three-path lifecycle summary

The Task collapses three lifecycle paths into the same TaskRun shape:

| Path | Trigger | What the Task does |
|------|---------|---------------------|
| **Create** | First run for the tenant. | Creates user, grants `provisioning`, creates template, mints token, writes Connector + Secret. |
| **Refresh** | `user_tokens/search` finds the named token **AND** the tenant Secret still carries a non-empty `token` value, **AND** neither `tokenName` nor `tokenDuration` changed since last run. | Reuses the user, template, `provisioning` grant, and the existing token — no SonarQube-side write. Re-applies the Connector + Secret via SSA (no-op on byte-identical input). |
| **Recreate** | Any of: `user_tokens/search` misses, the tenant Secret is missing or empty, `tokenName` / `tokenDuration` changed. | Reuses the user and template. Revokes the old token if present, then mints a fresh one (`expirationDate = today UTC + tokenDuration`). Re-applies the Connector + Secret. |

Note: the Task does **not** inspect the token's `expirationDate` —
SonarQube has no token-renew API and the Task does not poll for expiry.
If a token in `user_tokens/search` is expired but still present, the
Task treats it as reused; the expiry surfaces at scan time as a `401`,
and the next Task run (search-hit but Secret retains the dead value)
will self-heal only if the operator clears the tenant Secret first. To
get pure cadence-driven rotation, schedule the TaskRun on a cron and
pair it with a deliberate revoke (see
[Using the Task — Rotation](./using_sonarqube_connector_automatic_creation_task.mdx#rotation)).

All three paths are driven by the same params and exit with the same
results, so the same TaskRun can be scheduled to run on a cron without
case-splitting in the caller.

## Security notes

- **Admin token never leaves the workspace.** The Task reads admin
  credentials only via the `sonarqube-config` workspace (curl
  `--config` shape, or via the Connectors Proxy in Mode B). The admin
  token does not appear in any env var, command argument, or Pod
  spec.
- **Tenant token stored in tmpfs.** The freshly-minted token is
  written to `/workspace/secrets/token` on a `medium: Memory` volume,
  cleared by trap on step exit, and never logged.
- **Pod runs as non-root.** `runAsUser: 65532`,
  `runAsNonRoot: true`, `allowPrivilegeEscalation: false`, all
  capabilities dropped, `seccompProfile: RuntimeDefault`.
- **Verbose mode is safe to enable.** `verbose=true` enables shell
  tracing for the helper scripts, but credentials and tokens are
  redacted regardless of this flag.
- **Restrict who can trigger the TaskRun.** Triggering this Task mints
  (and rotates) SonarQube tokens and writes Secrets to the target
  namespace — gate it via Tekton RBAC on `TaskRun.create` plus
  workspace access.

## See also

- [Using the Task](./using_sonarqube_connector_automatic_creation_task.mdx) —
  prerequisites, onboarding, rotation, and offboarding.
- [SonarQube ConnectorClass](../concepts/sonarqube_connectorclass.mdx) —
  underlying Connector model used by the Task output.
- [Trouble shooting](../trouble_shooting/index.mdx) — diagnose
  Connector / proxy / SonarQube errors after the Task has run.
